_note: Outreach recipient database. Three plays: 'maintainer' (Play 1), 'charter' (Play 2), 'newsletter' (Play 3). Each recipient may have email=null if no public email exists — those need DM/contact-form outreach by the user manually. Verify name + company + funding stage are current before send. Last updated: 2026-05-17.

maintainer.name: João Moura
maintainer.contactNotes: DM on X @joaomdmoura, or use crewai.com contact form. Most brand-conscious of the bunch; most likely to engage.

vars.framework_adapter_status: v0.2 in flight; adapter scaffold pending review
vars.specific_open: Built and shipped przm in <3 weeks — CrewAI is the framework I think has the most to gain from a credible third-party number, and the most to lose from waiting.
vars.scores_line: (CrewAI adapter ships in v0.2 ~7-10 days; what's on the leaderboard today is baseline / AutoGen / gpt-5-mini / gpt-4o-mini)

maintainer.name: Harrison Chase
maintainer.contactNotes: Public email on LangChain materials. Least likely to engage (LangChain has dealt with critical benchmarks before). Pitch as 'most-cited framework in agent literature, want adapter to be fair'.

vars.framework_adapter_status: v0.2 in flight; LangGraph is on the next-up list given its citation share
vars.specific_open: LangGraph is the most-cited framework in published work on agent debate. That makes the fairness of our adapter implementation more important than for the others.

maintainer.name: Ilan Bigio
maintainer.contactNotes: DM on X @ibigio. OpenAI DX doesn't buy cert from third parties so SKIP the cert offer — frame as 'your number, your adapter, your feedback before we publish'.

vars.framework_adapter_status: Agents SDK adapter is v0.3 work; the closest thing in v0.1 is the baseline Anthropic adapter, which doesn't use a framework at all
vars.specific_open: I want to make sure the adapter we eventually run against the Agents SDK doesn't unfairly disadvantage your orchestration. Right now there's no adapter on the leaderboard — but when there is, it'd be useful to have your team's eyes on it first.
vars.scores_line: (no Agents SDK row on the leaderboard yet; v0.3)

maintainer.name: Microsoft Agent Framework team
maintainer.contactNotes: No individual maintainer on README. Use Discord office hours per their COMMUNITY.md (aka.ms/agent-framework-discord). Acknowledge AutoGen is in maintenance mode.

vars.framework_adapter_status: Agent Framework adapter is v0.3 work. AutoGen (the predecessor in maintenance mode) IS on the v0.1 leaderboard already with the headline finding.
vars.specific_open: AutoGen's RoundRobinGroupChat is the v0.1 standout finding: same model as the baseline, 7.3× lower collapse rate. When the Agent Framework adapter lands, that pattern is worth re-testing under the new orchestration.
vars.scores_line: AutoGen on gpt-4o-mini: 83.3% correct, 13.3% collapse rate — vs hand-rolled baseline on the same model at 96.7% collapse

maintainer.name: AutoGen community maintainers
maintainer.contactNotes: Discord (aka.ms/autogen-discord). AutoGen IS on the v0.1 leaderboard already. Maintenance-mode community may engage sincerely. Tone: collaborative.

vars.framework_adapter_status: AutoGen adapter shipped in v0.1 against the maintenance-mode 0.7.x release
vars.specific_open: Wanted to share the AutoGen numbers with the community before the public launch sequence kicks in — RoundRobinGroupChat ended up being the most convergence-resistant orchestration we tested.
vars.scores_line: AutoGen on gpt-4o-mini: 83.3% correct, 13.3% collapse rate, 0.0% sycophancy

charter.name: Mem0 team
charter.contactNotes: Need to look up DX/founders email on mem0.ai. Memory-axis cert: their pitch is memory recall — they're literally already on the memory benchmark.

vars.specific_reason: Mem0's memory-recall pitch deserves a third-party cert receipt, not a self-published number. The memory axis of przm already tracks you.

charter.name: Letta team
charter.contactNotes: Letta (formerly MemGPT). Look up founders email on letta.com.

vars.specific_reason: Letta competes on long-term memory + agent durability — both directly measured by przm. The signed receipt is something you can publish that customers can verify.

charter.name: Zep team
charter.contactNotes: YC W23. Look up founders email on getzep.com. Temporal-reasoning angle.

vars.specific_reason: LongMemEval-temporal is Zep's strongest axis on paper. A signed receipt makes that claim verifiable.

charter.name: CrewAI commercial team
charter.contactNotes: Same company as joaomdmoura but commercial side. Need to identify the right person (head of sales? VP of growth?). Use crewai.com contact form for now.

vars.specific_reason: Enterprise customers ask about agent reliability constantly. A signed convergence receipt is sales-side enablement you can cite in security questionnaires.

charter.name: Pinecone DevRel
charter.contactNotes: Series B. DevRel contact via pinecone.io. Their pitch is 'context for agents' — perfect angle.

vars.specific_reason: Pinecone's 'context for agents' positioning could use a third-party number that shows agents-on-Pinecone resist convergence collapse better than alternatives.

newsletter.name: Nathan Lambert
newsletter.contactNotes: Best fit. Covers methodology rigor + benchmarks + post-training. Cite the two arXiv papers (2509.23055 and 2508.14918).

vars.specific_open: Long-time Interconnects reader; the rigor with which you cover benchmark integrity is exactly what I want stress-tested on przm's methodology page.
vars.pitch_angle: Convergence + sycophancy benchmarking for multi-agent systems, deterministic scoring, Ed25519 signed receipts, adversarial holdouts (planned).

newsletter.name: Sahar Mor
newsletter.contactNotes: LinkedIn DM. Newsletter format is weekly news roundup — pitch as event, not essay.

vars.specific_open: AI Tidbits covers AI engineering news weekly — przm just shipped v0.1 of an AI reliability benchmark suite that may be a fit for the next roundup.
vars.pitch_angle: Open source benchmark for multi-agent AI failure modes with signed receipts. Real finding: 7.3× collapse-rate difference from orchestration alone, same model.

newsletter.name: Gergely Orosz
newsletter.contactNotes: pragmaticengineer.com newsletter pitch form. Long shot for v0.1 — better fit when we have an enterprise customer story.

vars.specific_open: Pragmatic Engineer covers engineering management decisions. przm gives AI engineering managers a third-party number to anchor framework-selection conversations.
vars.pitch_angle: How AI engineering teams choose between agent frameworks — and the data point nobody's measuring.
